task.prompt: When an onRoute hook is registered on the root Fastify instance after a plugin has already been registered, the hook is not called for routes added inside that plugin. Routes registered in encapsulated plugins silently skip globally-registered onRoute hooks that were added after the plugin's registration. This causes plugin-based route monitoring and instrumentation to silently miss routes. Investigate the hook inheritance mechanism across plugin encapsulation and fix the ordering issue.

hint_levels.symptom_only: Global lifecycle callbacks added to the server after a plugin is registered are silently skipped for routes inside that plugin. Route monitoring and instrumentation produces incomplete data — some routes are never reported.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug spans lib/hooks.js, lib/plugin-override.js, and lib/route.js. When a plugin is encapsulated, buildHooks() copies the current hooks at that point in time. onRoute hooks registered on the parent after encapsulation are not copied into the plugin scope.
hint_levels.function_hint: In lib/plugin-override.js line 38-47, buildHooks(instance[kHooks]) is called at plugin registration time. lib/hooks.js line 73-91 copies only the hooks that exist at that moment. Any onRoute hook added to the parent after this point is invisible to the plugin's route registration at lib/route.js line 295-301.

reasoning_chain: Read the symptom: onRoute hooks miss routes in encapsulated plugins
Open lib/plugin-override.js and find the encapsulation logic at line 38-47
Find buildHooks() call — hooks are copied at plugin creation time
Open lib/hooks.js and trace buildHooks() at line 73-91 — copies current hooks snapshot
Open lib/route.js and find route registration at line 295-301 — fires this[kHooks].onRoute
Realize: hooks added to parent AFTER plugin registration are not in the plugin's snapshot
Open fastify.js to understand the plugin lifecycle and avvio integration
Open lib/symbols.js to confirm kHooks symbol usage across files
Apply fix: use a reference to parent hooks for onRoute instead of a copied snapshot, or rebuild the hook chain at route-registration time
